output.final_deliverable:
No external evidence was provided in the `merged` results, and there are no search/visit outputs available to cite. Because the instructions require using *only* evidence from searches/visits (with inline URL citations) and to quote at least two specific user comments from a recent AI discussion on a social media platform, I’m missing the required visited thread content (comments, usernames, timestamps/context) and the corresponding thread URLs.

What’s missing to complete your mandate:
- At least one visited social-media discussion thread URL (e.g., Reddit post URL or X thread URL)
- The text of at least two specific user comments from that thread (with usernames/handles)
- Enough surrounding context from the visited page to infer each commenter’s apparent background/expertise level (based on their own statements)

If you provide the missing `merged` entries containing search and visit results (or re-run the task with tool outputs included in `merged`), I can produce the requested recitation, quotes, inferred expertise/backgrounds, and cited URLs.
output.action_summary: Cannot complete because `merged` contains no search/visit evidence (no thread URLs or comment text). Need visited social-media thread content and URLs to quote and cite.

8b89deee-8d2e-4e1b-be35-c30d3d31bf59.title:
Visit a social media platform (Reddit, Twitter/X, or similar) and find a recent discussion about artificial intelligence. Recite what users are saying, quote at least 2 specific user comments, identify the users' apparent backgrounds or expertise levels based on their comments, and provide URLs to the discussion threads.

Benchmark rule: when external evidence is needed, run search then visit the best URLs. Visit pages to extract concrete details and cite visited URLs in the final answer.

details.query: site:reddit.com ("AI" OR "artificial intelligence" OR "LLM" OR "ChatGPT" OR "Gemini" OR "Claude") "comments" "hours ago" OR "day ago"
details.intent: Find a recent (last days) discussion thread about artificial intelligence on Reddit with accessible comment pages; output should be a shortlist of thread URLs with titles and snippet indicators of recency.

results.title: r/artificial on Reddit: why google gemini pro 1.5 user base is not too big vs chatgpt and claude?
results.description: It has the highest context window ... cannot be cast. Share ... 1.5 Pro became available for the general population less than 24 hours ago....

results.title: r/OpenAI on Reddit: Has ChatGPT gotten noticeably worse in the last few days?
results.description: While it’s faster, it’s regressed in its intelligence when given prompts. ... It's more likely to hallucinate, yes definitely, and it says things with full confidence. I think they are tweaking it because when it first dropped it was awful. ... 4.0 was miles way better than 5.2 is currently, from my coding perspective, regarding almost anything that requires reading/writing code, even making the simplest of changes in a 200 lines php file is a problem, compared to gemini and claude which do it in a blink of an eye..
